intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid transitions when data refreshes rather than instant redraws, creating a sense of continuous flow in health tracking
facade.axisTargeted: Transitions: fluid animations vs instant updates
facade.label: Smooth slide-in
facade.acceptImplies: Favor animated state changes, easing functions, and graceful data transitions throughout the dashboard
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant state changes, snappy responses, and minimal animation overhead for a fast-feeling interface

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid transitions when data refreshes rather than instant redraws, creating a sense of continuous flow in health tracking
evidence.implication: Favor animated state changes, easing functions, and graceful data transitions throughout the dashboard

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable shapes over sharp angles for health tracking interfaces
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Build with generous border-radius, curved buttons, pill-shaped badges, smooth containers
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer cleaner geometry—consider right angles, minimal curves, or geometric precision

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer friendly, encouraging micro-interactions over clinical data entry
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality — playful vs serious
facade.label: Cheerful check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should use warm language, celebratory feedback, and motivational tone when logging health data
facade.rejectImplies: The dashboard should use neutral, factual language and minimal personality in its interface and messaging

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer aggregated metrics (one number per metric) over time-series detail for glanceable health overviews
facade.axisTargeted: aggregation level — granular data vs rolled-up snapshots
facade.label: Weekly summary card
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should lead with key totals and trends; drill-down optional. Users value at-a-glance insight over data density.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want to see raw data, full history, or granular detail without summarization. Dashboard should default to richness over brevity.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer clean, spacious card layouts over dense information density when tracking health data over time
facade.label: Minimal data cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward breathing room, whitespace, and restrained visual hierarchy—suggest dashboard with large cards, one metric per card, generous margins
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer information-rich density, dashboard-style compression, or multiple metrics visible at once—explore compact tile grids or layered information panels

facade.hypothesis: Users track experiments better with persistent left navigation paired with scrollable card summaries rather than tabs across the top
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Vertical sidebar remains during scroll; experiment cards stack below with key metrics visible at a glance; reduces cognitive load of tab-switching
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers alternative layout structure — perhaps top tabs, list view, or different information grouping; signals priority toward horizontal space or different scanning pattern

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users expect swipe and drag gestures to fetch updated health metrics, rather than requiring explicit button taps or page refreshes, making data interaction feel continuous and embodied
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — gesture-driven vs click-driven interaction
facade.label: Gesture pulls data
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize gesture affordances (swipe, drag, pull-to-refresh) for dashboard interactions; embed data fetching in natural finger movements rather than discrete button states
facade.rejectImplies: Stick to explicit, click-based controls for data refresh; gestures are secondary or absent; each action requires intentional, discrete user input

draft.title: Health Experiment Tracker
draft.summary: A personal dashboard for monitoring long-term health experiments with experiment cards, metrics, and progress visualization.
draft.html:
Health Experiments

Track your long-term wellness initiatives

3
Active
12
Days Avg

Current Experiments

Morning Meditation

10 minutes daily practice

Streak 18 days
Completion 65%

Sleep Tracking

Monitor sleep quality and duration

Streak 24 days
Completion 82%

Water Intake

8 glasses per day target

Streak 7 days
Completion 45%
